metadata.description: Evaluates whether a reasonable accommodation must be provided to a qualified individual with a disability. Runs accommodation request validation (rule_based), undue hardship analysis (standards_based), direct threat analysis (standards_based), and produces a composite accommodation determination. Depends on ada_coverage_eligibility outputs.

dependency_inputs.description: These fields are consumed from Module 1 (ada_coverage_eligibility) outputs. Module 2 does not re-evaluate them but uses them for short-circuit logic in component 2.4.

accommodation_rights_attach.name: accommodation_rights_attach
accommodation_rights_attach.description: False for regarded_as-only coverage. Module 2 must not proceed to accommodation analysis when this is false.

accommodation_request.description: Validates whether a proper accommodation request has been made. No 'magic words' are required — the individual need only make the employer aware that an adjustment or modification is needed due to a medical condition. Requests may be oral or written, and may be made by the employee, a representative, or a medical provider. An employer may also initiate the interactive process upon observing performance issues that may have a medical basis.
accommodation_request.source_citation: 29 CFR § 1630.2(o); 29 CFR § 1630.9; EEOC Enforcement Guidance No. 915.002 §§ 1–4

accommodation_type_requested.name: accommodation_type_requested
accommodation_type_requested.description: The category of accommodation being requested. Use canonical vocabulary term.

accommodation_description.name: accommodation_description
accommodation_description.description: Freeform description of the specific accommodation requested (e.g., 'ergonomic chair with lumbar support', '4 weeks unpaid medical leave'). Supplements the category for reasoning output.

accommodation_request_date.name: accommodation_request_date
accommodation_request_date.description: Date on which the accommodation was first requested or the employer first became aware of the need.

request_made_by.name: request_made_by
request_made_by.description: The party who made the accommodation request. Employees, their representatives, and their healthcare providers may all initiate a valid request. Employers may initiate the interactive process upon observing performance issues with a possible medical basis.

accommodation_is_for.name: accommodation_is_for
accommodation_is_for.description: The purpose for which the accommodation is requested — application process, performing job functions, or accessing benefits and privileges of employment.

functional_limitation_identified.name: functional_limitation_identified
functional_limitation_identified.description: True if the request identifies (even informally) the functional limitation or workplace barrier the accommodation is meant to address. A request must do more than state a diagnosis — it must connect the condition to a need for modification. However, no technical precision is required.

accommodation_request_in_writing.name: accommodation_request_in_writing
accommodation_request_in_writing.description: True if the accommodation request was made or confirmed in writing. Writing is not required by law, but its presence or absence is relevant to documentation of the interactive process.

accommodation_previously_denied.name: accommodation_previously_denied
accommodation_previously_denied.description: True if the employer has previously denied this or a substantially similar accommodation request. Relevant to pattern-of-denial analysis in Module 4 (not evaluated here, but captured for completeness).

accommodation_request_valid.name: accommodation_request_valid
accommodation_request_valid.description: True if the accommodation request meets the threshold requirements for a valid ADA request.

accommodation_category.name: accommodation_category
accommodation_category.description: The canonical accommodation type category as validated against vocabulary.

request_validity_basis.name: request_validity_basis
request_validity_basis.description: The specific basis for the validity determination.

reasoning.name: reasoning
reasoning.description: Plain-language explanation of the request validity determination with source citations.

undue_hardship_analysis.description: Evaluates whether the proposed accommodation would impose undue hardship — significant difficulty or expense — on the covered entity. All five statutory factor groups under 29 CFR § 1630.2(p) must be assessed. The employer bears the burden of proof. A generalized claim of expense is insufficient; documented, individualized analysis is required. Cost burden ratio is calculated as net_cost / denominator and rounded to 2 decimal places.
undue_hardship_analysis.cost_burden_ratio_note: Calculated as: accommodation_net_cost / (facility_operating_budget if available, else facility_annual_revenue). Rounded to 2 decimal places. Expressed as a percentage. If denominator is 0 or missing, ratio is null and added to key_uncertainties.

accommodation_gross_cost.name: accommodation_gross_cost
accommodation_gross_cost.description: Total gross cost of providing the accommodation before any tax credits, deductions, or outside funding. In USD.

tax_credits_available.name: tax_credits_available
tax_credits_available.description: Dollar value of available tax credits (e.g., IRS Disabled Access Credit for small businesses, barrier removal deduction) that offset accommodation cost. Default 0.

outside_funding_available.name: outside_funding_available
outside_funding_available.description: Dollar value of outside funding sources (e.g., state vocational rehabilitation funding, grants) that offset accommodation cost. Default 0.

accommodation_net_cost.name: accommodation_net_cost
accommodation_net_cost.description: Net cost of accommodation after credits and outside funding. If provided, validated against: gross_cost - tax_credits - outside_funding. If not provided, calculated by the engine. Per 29 CFR § 1630.2(p)(2)(i), net cost is the operative cost figure for undue hardship analysis.

accommodation_frequency.name: accommodation_frequency
accommodation_frequency.description: Whether the accommodation cost is a one-time expense, periodic, or recurring.

facility_annual_revenue.name: facility_annual_revenue
facility_annual_revenue.description: Annual revenue of the specific facility where the employee works. Used as cost burden denominator when operating budget is unavailable. Per 29 CFR § 1630.2(p)(2)(ii).

facility_employee_count.name: facility_employee_count
facility_employee_count.description: Number of employees at the specific facility. Per 29 CFR § 1630.2(p)(2)(ii).

facility_operating_budget.name: facility_operating_budget
facility_operating_budget.description: Annual operating budget of the specific facility, if available. Preferred denominator for cost burden ratio when available over facility_annual_revenue.

entity_annual_revenue.name: entity_annual_revenue
entity_annual_revenue.description: Total annual revenue of the covered entity (parent organization). Per 29 CFR § 1630.2(p)(2)(ii).

entity_total_employees.name: entity_total_employees
entity_total_employees.description: Total number of employees across all facilities of the covered entity. Per 29 CFR § 1630.2(p)(2)(ii).

entity_number_of_facilities.name: entity_number_of_facilities
entity_number_of_facilities.description: Total number of facilities operated by the covered entity. Informs entity type classification and undue hardship analysis.

entity_type.name: entity_type
entity_type.description: Structural classification of the entity. Used in assessing geographic separateness and resource availability.

administrative_relationship.name: administrative_relationship
administrative_relationship.description: Degree of administrative and fiscal integration between the facility and the parent entity. Affects whether entity-level resources are attributed to the facility for hardship analysis.

geographic_separateness.name: geographic_separateness
geographic_separateness.description: True if the facility is geographically remote from the entity's other facilities or headquarters. Geographic separateness weighs toward treating the facility as a more independent unit for hardship analysis.

workforce_composition_description.name: workforce_composition_description
workforce_composition_description.description: Freeform description of the workforce composition and structure at the facility (e.g., 'all employees work on production floor', 'mix of office and remote workers'). Context for impact analysis.

impact_on_operations.name: impact_on_operations
impact_on_operations.description: Assessment of the accommodation's impact on the facility's ability to conduct business.

impact_on_other_employees.name: impact_on_other_employees
impact_on_other_employees.description: Assessment of the accommodation's impact on other employees' ability to perform their duties.

impact_description.name: impact_description
impact_description.description: Freeform description of the operational or employee impact (e.g., 'three co-workers must cover additional shifts', 'elevator installation blocks loading dock for 3 months').

employer_asserts_hardship.name: employer_asserts_hardship
employer_asserts_hardship.description: True if the employer is asserting undue hardship as a defense. If false, undue_hardship_applies is automatically no regardless of cost factors.

employer_documented_hardship_analysis.name: employer_documented_hardship_analysis
employer_documented_hardship_analysis.description: True if the employer has produced a documented, individualized analysis of the hardship factors. Per 29 CFR § 1630.15(d), the employer bears the burden of proving undue hardship. A generalized assertion without documentation is insufficient to meet this burden.

alternative_accommodation_considered.name: alternative_accommodation_considered
alternative_accommodation_considered.description: True if the employer has considered and documented alternative, lower-cost accommodations before asserting hardship. Failure to consider alternatives weighs against a hardship finding.

undue_hardship_analysis.name: undue_hardship_analysis
undue_hardship_analysis.description: Detailed factor analysis of the undue hardship determination.

cost_burden_ratio.name: cost_burden_ratio
cost_burden_ratio.description: Net accommodation cost as a percentage of the denominator (facility operating budget or annual revenue). Rounded to 2 decimal places. Null if denominator is 0 or missing.

cost_burden_denominator_used.name: cost_burden_denominator_used
cost_burden_denominator_used.description: Identifies which denominator was used for the cost burden ratio.

factors_for_hardship.name: factors_for_hardship
factors_for_hardship.description: Factors supporting a finding of undue hardship. Alphabetical order.

factors_against_hardship.name: factors_against_hardship
factors_against_hardship.description: Factors against a finding of undue hardship. Alphabetical order.

key_uncertainties.name: key_uncertainties
key_uncertainties.description: Specific information gaps affecting the hardship determination. Required when undue_hardship_applies is indeterminate.

alternative_accommodations_available.name: alternative_accommodations_available
alternative_accommodations_available.description: True if the engine identifies potentially lower-cost alternative accommodations the employer should consider before asserting hardship.

alternative_accommodations_suggested.name: alternative_accommodations_suggested
alternative_accommodations_suggested.description: List of alternative accommodation options to explore if primary request poses hardship.

reasoning.name: reasoning
reasoning.description: Plain-language explanation distinguishing factual findings from legal conclusions, with source citations. Must address: (1) net cost and cost burden ratio, (2) entity/facility resource context, (3) operational impact, (4) employer burden of proof.

direct_threat_analysis.description: Evaluates whether the individual poses a direct threat — a significant risk of substantial harm to health or safety that cannot be eliminated or reduced to an acceptable level by reasonable accommodation. The assessment must be individualized, based on current medical knowledge and objective evidence, and must consider all four statutory factors from 29 CFR § 1630.2(r). Reliance on stereotypes, generalizations, or speculation is a procedural defect that defeats the defense regardless of substantive factors. Direct threat is a narrow exception; the logic skews toward 'no' or 'indeterminate' unless all factors clearly support the defense.

direct_threat_claimed.name: direct_threat_claimed
direct_threat_claimed.description: True if the employer is asserting direct threat as a defense to the accommodation obligation. If false, direct_threat_exists is automatically no and remaining inputs are not required.

threat_to.name: threat_to
threat_to.description: Who the alleged direct threat is directed at.

risk_duration.name: risk_duration
risk_duration.description: Duration profile of the alleged risk. Per 29 CFR § 1630.2(r) factor 1.

potential_harm_severity.name: potential_harm_severity
potential_harm_severity.description: Severity of the potential harm. Per 29 CFR § 1630.2(r) factor 2.

harm_likelihood.name: harm_likelihood
harm_likelihood.description: Likelihood that the potential harm will actually occur, based on objective evidence. Per 29 CFR § 1630.2(r) factor 3.

harm_imminence.name: harm_imminence
harm_imminence.description: Imminence of the potential harm. Per 29 CFR § 1630.2(r) factor 4.

individualized_assessment_performed.name: individualized_assessment_performed
individualized_assessment_performed.description: True if the employer conducted an individualized assessment of this specific employee's current ability to safely perform the job, rather than relying on generalizations about the condition. Procedural requirement under 29 CFR § 1630.2(r).

assessment_based_on_current_medical_know.name: assessment_based_on_current_medical_knowledge
assessment_based_on_current_medical_know.description: True if the individualized assessment used current medical knowledge or best available objective evidence about the condition. Procedural requirement under 29 CFR § 1630.2(r).

objective_evidence_exists.name: objective_evidence_exists
objective_evidence_exists.description: True if objective, documented evidence (medical records, expert opinion, incident history) supports the threat assessment.

evidence_description.name: evidence_description
evidence_description.description: Freeform description of the objective evidence relied upon (e.g., 'neurologist letter documenting uncontrolled seizures', 'OSHA incident reports', 'DOT medical disqualification').

threat_can_be_mitigated_by_accommodation.name: threat_can_be_mitigated_by_accommodation
threat_can_be_mitigated_by_accommodation.description: True if a reasonable accommodation could reduce the risk to an acceptable level. If true, the direct threat defense fails — accommodation is the answer, not exclusion. Per 29 CFR § 1630.2(r).

mitigation_accommodation_description.name: mitigation_accommodation_description
mitigation_accommodation_description.description: Description of the specific accommodation(s) that could mitigate the alleged threat, if any exist.

assessment_relied_on_stereotypes.name: assessment_relied_on_stereotypes
assessment_relied_on_stereotypes.description: True if the employer's threat assessment relied on stereotypes, generalizations, or assumptions about the condition rather than individualized, evidence-based evaluation. A procedural defeater — automatically negates the direct threat defense per 29 CFR § 1630.2(r).

direct_threat_analysis.name: direct_threat_analysis
direct_threat_analysis.description: Detailed factor analysis of the direct threat determination.

factors_for_threat.name: factors_for_threat
factors_for_threat.description: Factors supporting a finding of direct threat. Alphabetical order.

factors_against_threat.name: factors_against_threat
factors_against_threat.description: Factors against a finding of direct threat. Alphabetical order.

mitigation_possible.name: mitigation_possible
mitigation_possible.description: True if a reasonable accommodation could reduce the threat to an acceptable level.

assessment_methodology_adequate.name: assessment_methodology_adequate
assessment_methodology_adequate.description: True if the employer's assessment met the procedural requirements of 29 CFR § 1630.2(r): individualized, current medical knowledge, objective evidence, no stereotypes.

key_uncertainties.name: key_uncertainties
key_uncertainties.description: Specific information gaps affecting the determination.

reasoning.name: reasoning
reasoning.description: Plain-language explanation of the direct threat determination. Must address: (1) adequacy of assessment methodology, (2) all four statutory factors, (3) whether mitigation was considered.

accommodation_determination.description: Aggregates all upstream determinations — Module 1 coverage/eligibility outputs plus components 2.1–2.3 — into a final accommodation obligation determination. Applies short-circuit logic in a fixed priority order. If all threshold checks pass and no defenses apply, the accommodation is required. Indeterminate propagates from any unresolved upstream component.
short_circuit_evaluation_order: 4. accommodation_rights_attach == false → no, not_disabled (regarded_as only; no RA obligation)
9. All checks pass, undue_hardship_applies == no, direct_threat_exists == no → yes
10. Any upstream indeterminate, no short-circuit fired → indeterminate

accommodation_required.name: accommodation_required
accommodation_required.description: Final determination of whether the employer must provide the accommodation.

accommodation_type_approved.name: accommodation_type_approved
accommodation_type_approved.description: The accommodation type that must be provided, populated when accommodation_required is yes.

denial_basis.name: denial_basis
denial_basis.description: The legal basis for denial, populated when accommodation_required is no.

factors_summary.name: factors_summary
factors_summary.description: Aggregated factor summary from upstream components.

factors_for_accommodation.name: factors_for_accommodation
factors_for_accommodation.description: Key factors from upstream components supporting the accommodation obligation.

factors_against_accommodation.name: factors_against_accommodation
factors_against_accommodation.description: Key factors from upstream components against the accommodation obligation.

recommended_actions.name: recommended_actions
recommended_actions.description: Ordered list of recommended next steps for the employer based on the determination.

reasoning.name: reasoning
reasoning.description: Plain-language summary of the composite accommodation determination citing applicable source sections.

sources_cited.name: sources_cited
sources_cited.description: Source citations supporting the composite determination.
